questions.question: Why did Sue send for the doctor when Johnsy fell ill?
questions.answer: Sue sent for the doctor because Johnsy was seriously ill with pneumonia and her condition was worsening.

questions.question: What did the doctor say about Johnsy’s chances of recovery?
questions.answer: The doctor said Johnsy could recover only if she had the will to live.

questions.question: Why did Sue start painting in Johnsy’s room?
questions.answer: Sue started painting in Johnsy’s room to distract her from her illness and negative thoughts.

questions.question: Why was Johnsy counting the falling leaves?
questions.answer: Johnsy believed that she would die when the last leaf fell from the ivy creeper.

questions.question: How did Sue react when Johnsy said she would die?
questions.answer: Sue tried to encourage her and told her that her belief was foolish.

questions.question: Why did Sue call Behrman upstairs?
questions.answer: Sue called Behrman to pose as a model for her painting.

questions.question: How did Behrman react when he heard about Johnsy’s belief?
questions.answer: He became angry and said she was foolish to think such things.

questions.question: What did Sue and Behrman notice outside the window?
questions.answer: They noticed that only one leaf remained on the ivy creeper.

questions.question: What was the weather like on the night the leaf was painted?
questions.answer: There was a storm with heavy rain and strong winds.

questions.question: What surprised Johnsy the next morning?
questions.answer: She was surprised to see that the last leaf had not fallen.

questions.question: How did Johnsy’s attitude change after seeing the leaf?
questions.answer: She began to regain hope and decided to live.

questions.question: What did Johnsy realize after observing the leaf?
questions.answer: She realized that it was wrong to wish for death.

questions.question: What did Sue give Johnsy after she felt better?
questions.answer: Sue gave her hot soup and a mirror.

questions.question: What did Johnsy do after looking in the mirror?
questions.answer: She combed her hair and smiled.

questions.question: What did the doctor say during his second visit?
questions.answer: He said Johnsy would recover because she had regained her will to live.

questions.question: Who else was suffering from pneumonia in the building?
questions.answer: Behrman was suffering from pneumonia.

questions.question: How long did Behrman remain ill?
questions.answer: He remained ill for only two days.

questions.question: What did the janitor find in Behrman’s room?
questions.answer: He found Behrman lying ill with wet clothes and shoes.

questions.question: What objects were found near Behrman’s bed?
questions.answer: A ladder and a lantern were found near his bed.

questions.question: What paints were found near the ladder?
questions.answer: Green and yellow paints were found.

questions.question: What truth did Sue finally reveal to Johnsy?
questions.answer: Sue revealed that the last leaf was painted by Behrman.

questions.question: Why did Behrman paint the leaf?
questions.answer: He painted it to give Johnsy hope and save her life.

questions.question: What qualities of Sue are shown in the story?
questions.answer: Sue is caring, loyal, and supportive.

questions.question: What theme is highlighted in the story?
questions.answer: The story highlights hope, friendship, and sacrifice.

questions.question: Why is Behrman’s painting called a masterpiece?
questions.answer: Because it saved Johnsy’s life by giving her hope.
